prompt:
Loading the model fails with the following error on a T4 GPU pod. Script and traceback below.

```python
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3',
                                              torch_dtype='bfloat16',
                                              attn_implementation='flash_attention_2')
```

Traceback:
```
ValueError: FlashAttention only supports Ampere GPUs or newer.
```

criteria.description: Identifies the actual root cause, not a downstream symptom
Suggests a fix that would actually resolve it
Does not introduce a new bug in the fix
Explains why the bug happened (mechanistic understanding)

reference_solution:
Root cause: FlashAttention-2 requires GPUs with Ampere architecture or newer (sm_80+) - that's A100, A10, RTX-30/40 series, H100, etc. The T4 is Turing architecture (sm_75) and lacks the tensor-core primitives FA2's kernels depend on. The check fails at model-load time during the attention-implementation dispatch.

Fix: pick an attention implementation supported on the available hardware. For Turing-class GPUs:
```python
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3',
                                              torch_dtype='float16',
                                              attn_implementation='sdpa')
```
Note two changes: (1) `attn_implementation='sdpa'` uses PyTorch's built-in scaled-dot-product-attention which has math/mem-efficient kernels supported on Turing+; (2) `torch_dtype='float16'` because T4 has limited bf16 support - fp16 is the practical dtype on Turing. If you must use bf16, fall back to `attn_implementation='eager'` and accept the speed/memory hit.

Mechanism: FlashAttention kernels are hand-tuned CUDA C++ that uses Ampere's larger shared-memory + asynchronous-copy instructions to fuse softmax with the matmul, dramatically reducing HBM bandwidth pressure. Those instructions don't exist on pre-Ampere GPUs. PyTorch's SDPA implementation includes a memory-efficient kernel (Xformers-derived) that gives ~70% of FA2's speedup on supported hardware and works on Turing+ via tensor-cores. For a CI / smoke-test pod, sdpa is the right default; for production training, ensure the pod is Ampere or newer.
